body.title: Find and stop failover_tool channel spam — poisons Igor's NE
body.result: root cause: test_failover.py and test_comms_default_channels.py wrote to real Postgres on every pytest run; fixed by patching _comms=None and unsetting IGOR_HOME_DB_URL in affected tests
body.description:
Since 2026-04-18, appoint_home_db() fires on akiendelllinux (~30-40s after 'hello from shared' channel messages) and posts JSON to infra.channel_messages with author='failover_tool'. Igor's NE reads these as 'database errors are happening', generating high-salience NARRATIVE_GAP entries and ACTION_IMPULSE loops that displace real work.

Known facts:
- old_host is always '127.0.0.1' → call originates on akiendelllinux
- Fires in pairs: one for akiendell (10.0.0.99) and one for akienyoga9i (10.0.0.90)
- Consistent 30-40s delay after 'hello from shared' messages → something is watching the channel
- No cron job calls it; not in Igor tool registry; no CC hooks call it; no source code outside failover.py references it
- Old UC server log (~/.TheIgors/local/logs/utility_closet.log, up to May 8) shows the calls; current log does not
- There is an idle CC session in tmux session '0' running since 2026-05-11 in ~/TheIgors with 'Remote Control active' — likely the remote entry point from St. Louis trips
- Three tmux sessions: claude-main (this CC), igor (Igor process), 0 (old CC session)

**Hypothesis to investigate:** The tmux session '0' CC session has a prior conversation context or tool in flight that calls appoint_home_db when triggered. Alternatively, some background process (not cron, not Igor, not UC server) watches the channel and fires the calls.

**Affected files:** Investigation only — no file changes until source is identified
**Design rules:** none
**Scope boundary:** In scope: find the caller, stop it, optionally add a filter to protect Igor during the investigation period. Out of scope: removing the failover.py tool itself (it's legitimate infrastructure for real failover).
**Test plan:** After fix: send 'hello from shared' to channel, confirm no failover_tool messages appear within 60s.
